description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's services being flooded with requests.

steps.text: Disable the feature flag (loadGeneratorFloodHomepage) by manually editing the contents of the ConfigMap
Restart all of the Deployment workloads.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `ad` service experiencing high CPU usage.

steps.text: Disable the feature flag (adHighCpu) by manually editing the contents of the ConfigMap
Restart all of the Deployment workloads.

description: This scenario changes the environment variables of OpenTelemetry Demo's `shipping` service, causing it to be unable to reach the `quote` service.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the environment variable(s) value with the correct value(s).

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `checkout` service experiencing a failure.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule otel-demo-product-catalog-network-delay experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule otel-demo-product-catalog-network-delay experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `product-catalog` service using the wrong image, with a forced restart to trigger complete service outage.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid image with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `checkout` service being unable to start due to an unsupported architecture image, with a forced restart to trigger complete service outage.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid image with the correct value.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the node selector to allow Kubernetes to schedule the workload on a supported node (if exists).

description: This scenario simulates tampered HTTP requests to OpenTelemetry Demo's `email` service.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule email-http-chaos-post-body-tamper experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule email-http-chaos-post-body-tamper experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.

description: This scenario simulates aborted HTTP requests to OpenTelemetry Demo's `quote` service.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule quote-http-chaos-abort experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule quote-http-chaos-abort experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.

description: This scenario simulates a service port misconfiguration in OpenTelemetry Demo's checkout service, combined with network disruption to force connection recycling.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and replace the target port (9999) with the correct value (8080).

description: This scenario simulates all ingress traffic being blocked on OpenTelemetry Demo's `frontend` service.

steps.text: Retrieve and review the configuration of the network policy(s) in the affected namespace.
Manually edit the manifest(s) and allow access to workload's ports of the network policy(s).
Retrieve and review the configuration of the network policy(s) in the affected namespace.
Delete the offending network policy(s) if it is no longer required.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `ad` and `cart` services being deployed on the wrong node.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the node selector with the correct value.
Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the node selector with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates the wrong password being set as the user for OpenTelemetry Demo's `valkey-cart` service.

steps.text: Change the password back to the original by modifying the value of `valkey-password` in the secrete.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo services running normally with autoscaling.
This scenario simulates several OpenTelemetry Demo services being incorrectly autoscaled.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and replace the utilization values with the correct values.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the utilization values with the correct values.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `payment` service being unable to schedule a new pod.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the node selector with the correct value.
Retrieve all worker node nodes
Uncordon all worker nodes which are cordoned.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `valkey-cart` service experiencing an Out of Memory (OOM) error.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the code change volumes.
Delete the injected ConfigMap object.
Manually edit the manifest and change the script.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `cart` service consuming most of the node's available memory.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule cart-stress-chaos-memory experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule cart-stress-chaos-memory experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.
Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid container's resource configuration with the correct value(s).

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `payment` service being evicted from the node.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the node selector value with the correct value(s).
Create a new priority class with a higher value than the workload which caused its preemption.
Manually edit the manifest and change the priority of the workload to the new priority.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `frontend` service being reach other services.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid rule with the correct value.
Delete the faulty authorization policy.

description: This scenario simulates new pods of OpenTelemetry Demo's `payment` service being unscheduleable.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the faulty pod anti-affinity rule with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates new pods of OpenTelemetry Demo's `email` service being unable to execute.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the command of the hanging init container to one which will complete.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the faulty init container.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `postgresql` service not having enough resources.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid container's resource configuration with the correct value(s).

description: This scenario simulates BookInfo's `gateway` service unable to receive GET HTTP requests.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid rule with the correct value.
Delete the faulty authorization policy.

description: This scenario simulates BookInfo's `gateway` service unable to receive GET HTTP requests.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid label with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `cart` service having a malformed readiness probe.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the faulty readiness probe in the container rule.

description: This scenario simulates BookInfo's `productpage-v1` service being unable to communicate with other services.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest and to add the required Istio ambient mode labels to the workload.
Manually edit the namespace manifest and to add the required Istio ambient mode labels to the workload.

description: This scenario simulates Kubernetes API server a surge in request during OpenTelemetry Demo's execution.

steps.text: List all deployments in the namespace to identify the load generator.
Delete the workload-scanner deployment that is generating the API load.
Clean up the associated ClusterRole.
Clean up the associated ClusterRoleBinding.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo being unable to scale up with load is surging.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest(s) and increase the values of the resource quota(s).
Delete the offending resource quota(s) if it is no longer required.
Disable the feature flag (loadGeneratorFloodHomepage) by manually editing the contents of the ConfigMap
Restart all of the Deployment workloads.

description: This scenario simulates BookInfo's `reviews-v3` service being unable to start due to lack of persistent storage.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the `storageClassName` with the correct value.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the volume claim with an existing persistent volume claim.

description: This scenario simulates Kubernetes API server being stressed during OpenTelemetry Demo's execution.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule api-server-memory-stress experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule api-server-memory-stress experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.

description: This scenario simulates Kubernetes API server experiencing latency during OpenTelemetry Demo's execution.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh annotate schedule api-server-memory-stress experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Annotate the schedule to pause the active experiment.
Retrieve the associated experiment managed by the schedule.
Delete the retrieved experiement after it has entered the `Paused` state.

steps.command: kubectl -n chaos-mesh delete schedule api-server-memory-stress experiment.chaos-mesh.org/pause='true'
steps.text: Delete the schedule.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `product-catalog` service using the wrong image, testing Kubernetes rolling update resilience without forced restart.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid image with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `checkout` service being unable to start due to an unsupported architecture image, testing Kubernetes rolling update resilience without forced restart.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid image with the correct value.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the node selector to allow Kubernetes to schedule the workload on a supported node (if exists).

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `ad` service unable to run due to a resource quota on the namespace.

steps.text: Manually edit the manifest(s) and increase the values of the resource quota(s).
Delete the offending resource quota(s) if it is no longer required.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `product-catalog` service having a malformed command.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid command with the correct value.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `recommendation` service crashing due to a malformed ConfigMap.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and remove the malformed ConfigMap volumes.
Delete the injected malformed ConfigMap object.
Manually edit the ConfigMap and reduce its size to acceptable limits.

description: This scenario simulates OpenTelemetry Demo's `accounting` service being scaled to zero replicas.

steps.text: Scale the workload back to 1 replica to restore service.
Revert the last change done to the manifest to restore the original replica count.

description: This scenario simulates the OpenTelemetry Demo's `recommendation` service using a modified image that causes request errors.

steps.text: Revert the last change done to the manifest.
Manually edit the manifest and replace the invalid image with the correct value.
